summary: This cluster examines thermophilic cyanobacteria from hot springs in Bakreswar, West Bengal, India, focusing on species such as *Synechococcus bigranulatus*, *S. lividus*, *Gloeocapsa muralis*, and *Phormidium frigidum*. A key finding across the research is a distinct positive correlation between higher water temperatures and an increased carotenoid-to-chlorophyll-a ratio, indicating a physiological adaptation to thermal stress. The studies also assessed the nitrogen-fixing capacity of these cyanobacteria and mapped their distribution patterns in relation to varying water temperature and pH across different spring locations.
